TTIS LMS — QMS Competence System-of-Record: Architecture & Schema (v3.0)

Doc ID: R-LMS-01 / QMS-COMP-01 Status: As-built design-review baseline · 2026-06-15. Covers R-LMS-01 (Q1–Q6), R-XC-01 (external credentials), R-COMP-01 (competency registry), and R-AA-01 P4a (three-stage approval gate + per-user capability grants) — all backend + frontend merged. Supersedes: v2.0 (Q1–Q6 only). v1.0 was the pre-build gap analysis. Source of truth (backend, repo ttis-ai): dashboard/lms.py (service + schema, 22 lms_* tables), dashboard/main.py (/api/v1/lms/*), dashboard/abac.py (capability PDP), dashboard/users.py (capability_grants), orchestration/audit_logger.py (SHA-256 hash chain). Front end (repo TTI-S_Dashboard): src/components/vbs/lms-*, src/components/admin/*, src/lib/vbs/{schemas,types,endpoints}.ts.

Reviewer's orientation. This is an in-house LMS fused into the TTIS VBS platform — not a standalone product and not a bridge to an external LMS. The fusion is deliberate: credentials inherit the platform's single identity and single tamper-evident audit chain, which is what makes them compliance-grade. §1 covers the coupling and what extraction would take.

0. Purpose & the distinction that drives the design

A QMS competence system-of-record for an SDVOSB low-voltage telecom contractor. It delivers training, assesses written knowledge and observed practical skill, issues earned, expiring, chain-anchored credentials (and records externally-earned ones by attestation), maps a role→competency matrix to readiness, decomposes competence into an auditable competency registry, recerts per-competency, and governs all curriculum content through a three-stage approval gate.

The auditor distinction — training tool vs. system of record — is the north star: - Every competence event (quiz attempt, course completion, practical assessment, credential issue/revoke/expire, approval decision, evidence-row change) is fail-closed chain-anchored into an append-only, verify_chain()-checkable log. - Evidence rows are WORM — no service/API path mutates or deletes them after the fact. - A documented retention floor (LMS_RETENTION_DAYS, default 2557 ≈ 7y) governs persistence. - Curriculum content cannot reach a learner without a competence-routed human gate, and each approval signature attests only to what its holder can verify (authority ≠ competence).

            ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 Frontend   │  Next.js dashboard (TTI-S_Dashboard) — Learning tabs + User-Admin       │

 (separate  │  Courses · Certifications · Assess · Review (gate) · Authoring (CEO)     │

  repo)     └───────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘

                                             │  HTTPS + per-user JWT

            ┌───────────────────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────────┐

 API        │  FastAPI :8000  dashboard/main.py   /api/v1/lms/*                        │

            │  require_capability(resource, Action) → ABAC PDP (dashboard/abac.py)     │

            └───────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘

 Domain     │  dashboard/lms.py  (auth-agnostic; takes by_actor; no abac import)       │

            │  catalog · delivery · assessment · certs/credentials · competency        │

            │  registry · matrix · recert · three-stage gate                           │

            └───────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────┘

 Data       │  SQLite/Postgres  lms.db (22 lms_* tables)         │ auth.db (capability_grants)

 Spine      │  orchestration/audit_logger.py — SHA-256 hash chain (audit.db), verify_chain()

            └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

1. System boundaries & coupling (the "is it separable?" question)

The LMS is a modular monolith inside VBS. Cleanly isolated: domain logic (one file, auth-agnostic — imports db only, never abac), data (own lms.db / lms_* namespace), API namespace (/api/v1/lms/*), UI components. Three platform seams bind it:

Seam Mechanism Notes
Auth / ABAC capabilities checked at the route layer (require_capability) domain is auth-agnostic — a clean seam
Audit hash-chain orchestration/audit_logger (log_event_strict, verify_chain) load-bearing & deliberate — the compliance backbone
Identity learners/assessors/reviewers are VBS users (auth.db) single user store, no parallel LMS identity

Standalone extraction is a bounded refactor, not a rewrite: abstract the three seams (inject an authz policy, carry/re-provide a tamper-evident log, supply a user store), split the route module + UI bundle. The audit-chain dependency is the one you would never drop.

Persistence/concurrency. db.get_connection("lms") → data/sqlite/lms.db locally / Cloud SQL Postgres in prod (DATABASE_URL); DDL dialect-portable (exec_ddl + _pg_adapt); all tables in init_lms_db(); column additions are idempotent ALTER … ADD COLUMN. Integer OCC version guards mutable parents (courses, enrollments, curricula, certifications, credentials, practicals, competencies).

2. Compliance spine

  • Fail-closed anchoring. _strict_anchor() wraps log_event_strict; a competence mutation that cannot be anchored fails rather than silently proceeding. Chain: entry_hash = SHA-256(msg_type ‖ payload_hash ‖ prev_hash), genesis GENESIS_BLOCK_TTIS_2026; verify_chain() recomputes the whole log and flags any altered/removed row. Config-level authoring uses best-effort log_event.
  • WORM evidence. Practicals lock on submit (locked=1); credentials transition forward only (active → expired|revoked); there is no service/API path that updates or deletes attempts, completions, credentials, practicals, or audit rows. Corrections are new linked records.
  • Row-level evidence audit (Q2). lms_record_audit appends a chain-anchored before/after row for every evidence mutation, with actor — so an app-path change is attributed and a direct-DB change is caught by verify_chain(). integrity_status() surfaces the live verdict; record_history() returns a record's trail.
  • Retention. RETENTION_DAYS (env, default 2557) is the stated floor; no hard-delete path exists.

3. Capability model (ABAC)

CEO = allow-all; auditor sub-role = read-only (denies all mutations, ahead of the CEO override); other roles get an explicit matrix. Per-user grants (R-AA-01 slice 0) overlay person-bound caps on top.

Capability Actions Held by Purpose
lms VIEW (all) · C/U/D (CEO) all view; authoring CEO catalog/authoring + learner surface
lms_assess VIEW, CREATE field_ops_mgr (+CEO) conduct practical assessments (Q1)
lms_extcred VIEW, CREATE field_ops_mgr (+CEO) record external credentials (R-XC-01); attestor ≠ learner
lms_approve_policy VIEW, APPROVE, REJECT field_ops_mgr (+CEO) Stage-2 gate, policy_anchored courses
lms_approve_trade VIEW, APPROVE, REJECT per-user grant Stage-2 gate, trade_technical; credential-gated (A4)
lms_approve_compliance VIEW, APPROVE, REJECT per-user grant Stage-2 gate, federal_compliance (FAR/CMMC)
lms_release APPROVE CEO (allow-all) Stage-3 release authorization
lms_author VIEW, CREATE authoring agent principal (+CEO) draft-only authoring (P4b)

Per-user grants overlay. Principal.grants (frozenset of (resource, action)) is loaded per request in get_current_principal (immediate revoke; not in the JWT). can() allows an action when the role matrix doesn't but an explicit grant does — after the auditor-deny and CEO override, so a grant can never subvert those. Store: capability_grants (auth.db); CEO-only, audit-anchored API GET/POST/DELETE /admin/users/{id}/grants; UI in User & Access Management.

4. Data model — full schema (22 lms_* tables + capability_grants)

TEXT typed-prefix ids; ISO-8601 UTC TEXT timestamps; INTEGER 0/1 booleans; integer OCC version; JSON arrays as TEXT. Bold = added since v2.0.

4.1 Catalog & delivery

lms_courses(course_id PK, code UNIQUE, title, description,

            status['draft'|'pending_review'|'release_pending'|'published'|'retired'],

            approval_track['policy_anchored'|'trade_technical'|'federal_compliance' DEFAULT trade_technical],  -- R-AA-01

            owner, version, created_by, created_at, updated_at)

lms_modules(module_id PK, course_id, title, position, created_at)

lms_lessons(lesson_id PK, module_id, course_id, title, position,

            content_type['native_md'|'video'|'document'|'scorm'|'xapi'], body, content_ref, created_at)

lms_enrollments(enrollment_id PK, learner_id, course_id, status['assigned'|'in_progress'|'completed'|'waived'],

                progress REAL, assigned_by, auto_basis, due_date, completed_at, chain_hash, version, created/updated_at)

lms_lesson_progress(enrollment_id, lesson_id, completed_at, PK(enrollment_id,lesson_id))

4.2 Assessment — written + practical (Q1)

lms_quizzes(quiz_id PK, course_id, title, passing_score REAL, attempt_limit INTEGER, created_at)

lms_questions(question_id PK, quiz_id, stem, qtype['mc'|'truefalse'|'multi'|'short'], points REAL,

              options(JSON), answer_key(JSON), position, competency_id)            -- competency_id: R-COMP-01

lms_attempts(attempt_id PK, enrollment_id, quiz_id, attempt_no, answers(JSON), score REAL, passed, chain_hash, created_at)

lms_rubrics(rubric_id PK, code UNIQUE, title, description, status, version, created_by, created/updated_at)

lms_rubric_criteria(criterion_id PK, rubric_id, ordinal, text, standard_ref, is_critical,

                    measure_type['observed'|'measured'|'document'], expected_value, competency_id)  -- R-COMP-01

lms_practical_assessments(assessment_id PK, learner_id, rubric_id, assessor_id, cert_id,

                          status['scheduled'|'in_progress'|'submitted'|'passed'|'failed'], overall_result,

                          assessor_attestation, started_at, decided_at, chain_hash, locked, version, created/updated_at)

lms_practical_results(result_id PK, assessment_id, criterion_id, outcome['pass'|'fail'|'na'], measured_value, note, captured_at)

4.3 Certifications & credentials (Q2 + R-XC-01)

lms_curricula(curriculum_id PK, code, title, description, course_ids(JSON[]), final_quiz_id, version, created/updated_at)

lms_certifications(cert_id PK, code, title, curriculum_id, validity_days INTEGER, recert_cadence, issuing_authority,

                   practical_rubric_id, standard_ref, issuance_mode['earned'|'external_attested'],  -- R-XC-01

                   version, created/updated_at)

lms_credentials(credential_id PK, learner_id, cert_id, issued_at, valid_until,

                status['active'|'expired'|'revoked'], certificate_hash, version, created/updated_at,

                issuance_mode, attested_by, external_provider, external_ref, attestation_note, evidence_uri)  -- R-XC-01

4.4 Competency registry (R-COMP-01)

lms_competencies(competency_id PK,                 -- '{CERT}-{TOPIC}-{NN}', e.g. FND-BP-04

                 code, title, description,

                 owning_course_id,                 -- EXACTLY ONE owner (no junction table = the enforcement)

                 cert_id, governing_standard_ref,  -- the clause

                 standard_authority['CSI'|'TIA'|'NEC'|'OSHA'|'FAR'|'CMMC'|'IEC'|'QMS'|'OTHER'],

                 is_critical, measure_type,

                 authored_by, source_refs, ratified_by, ratified_at,   -- provenance + M6 sign-off

                 version, created/updated_at)

lms_competency_prereqs(competency_id, requires_competency_id, created_at, PK(...))  -- cycle-guarded

4.5 Matrix, prerequisites, recert (Q4–Q6)

lms_competency_matrix(role, cert_id, required, grace_days, created/updated_at, PK(role,cert_id))  -- keyed by job titles

lms_prerequisites(course_id, requires_course_id, created_at, PK(course_id,requires_course_id))    -- course DAG

lms_standard_revisions(revision_id PK, standard_ref, revised_on, note, affected, created_at)

4.6 Approval gate + audit (R-AA-01 / Q2)

lms_approval_log(log_id PK, artifact_id, artifact_version, stage['track'|'release'], track, actor_id,

                 capability_used, reviewer_credential_id, reviewer_cred_type, reviewer_cred_ref,

                 reviewer_cred_valid_until,                          -- A7 credential capture (trade track)

                 action['approve'|'reject'|'release'|'override'], justification, chain_hash, created_at)

lms_record_audit(audit_id PK, record_table, record_id, action, actor, before_state(JSON), after_state(JSON), chain_hash, created_at)

4.7 Auth overlay (auth.db)

capability_grants(user_id, resource, action, granted_by, granted_at, PK(user_id,resource,action))

5. Competence layers (behavior)

  • Delivery. enroll (prereq-gated, idempotent) → mark_lesson_complete (progress rollup) → submit_attempt (server-scored: mc/truefalse/multi auto-graded, short manual; attempt-limited; anchored) → complete_course (server-verifies all lessons done + every quiz passed; anchored).
  • Earned credentials. Eligibility = all curriculum courses complete AND final assessment passed (if any) AND practical passed (if the cert sets practical_rubric_id) — so "passed the quiz = competent" is structurally impossible when a practical is required. issue_credential is server-verified, idempotent, anchored; valid_until from validity_days (NULL = permanent); expire_due_credentials is NULL-safe.
  • External credentials (R-XC-01). record_external_credential attests a credential earned outside the LMS (OSHA, BICSI, FOA, manufacturer). M4 mutual-exclusion wall: issue_credential refuses external_attested certs; record_external_credential refuses earned certs. Attestor ≠ learner (M5); dates are an attested claim (M7); fail-closed anchored. External certs carry no curriculum/rubric.
  • Competency registry (R-COMP-01). create_competency (one-owner + cert/owner consistency; external certs can't own competencies), list_competencies (filter by cert/course/authority/clause), update/ratify_competency (M6), competency-level prereqs, link_criterion/question_competency. competency_record (Q3) is a self-verifying per-person JSON: courses + written + practicals + credentials + per-competency clause + credential hash + a live verify_chain verdict.
  • Matrix & readiness (Q5). learner_readiness(role) → held/expired/missing per required cert; assign_required bulk-enrolls the courses behind a role's certs.
  • Recert (Q6). Each credential expires on its own clock; record_standard_revision additionally re-triggers recert on the citing certs only (per-competency, not a calendar reset); expiring_credentials is the reminder list.

6. The three-stage approval gate (R-AA-01)

No curriculum content reaches a learner without a competence-routed human gate. Authority is never a substitute for competence — each signature attests only to what its holder can verify.

 draft ──submit_for_review──▶ pending_review ──review_course(approve)──▶ release_pending ──record_release──▶ published

   ▲          (Stage 1: agent       │  Stage 2: TRACK GATE (competent      │   Stage 3: CEO release          │

   │           machine pre-review)  │  reviewer; capability by track)      │   (lms_release)                 │ retire

   └────reject(reason)──────────────┘                                      └──reject──▶ pending_review       ▼

                                                                                                        retired
  • Track routing (per-course approval_track, fail-safe default trade_technical): policy_anchored→lms_approve_policy · trade_technical→lms_approve_trade · federal_compliance→lms_approve_compliance. The required cap is checked in-handler (data-dependent).
  • A4 — credential-bound trade gate. A trade_technical approval is blocked unless the reviewer's own R-XC-01 credential is currently active; the credential (type/ref/validity-at-approval) is captured in lms_approval_log (A7) — the defensibility payload when a CO asks who stood behind a claim.
  • A6 — no silent override. The CEO may reject freely and release release_pending content; releasing content that did not pass Stage 2 is an override requiring a justification, logged with full attribution. Final authority is never a silent backdoor around the competent gate.
  • Enforcement boundary. POST /lms/courses/{id}/status refuses the gate-managed states (409) — the only externally-reachable publish path is the release chain. (Follow-up: extend the refusal to the set_course_status service primitive for defense-in-depth; it's retained today for trusted seeds.)
  • UI. Authoring console: a draft shows Submit for review (not Publish). Review tab (any approver): track-gate Approve/Reject (trade → reviewer-credential picker) and CEO Release.

7. Authoring agent (R-AA-01 P4b) — planned, not yet built

The AI curriculum authoring agent drafts content + assessment items for the content-empty course shells, grounded in the approved corpus, staged as draft for the gate. Locked mandates: reuse the existing Sonnet specialist service (roles curriculum_author + curriculum_compliance_reviewer, no new standalone service); grounded generation only (every standards claim carries ≥1 corpus provenance ref, else a flag); structured-output contract; draft-only (holds lms_author, never a publish/approve cap — the M9 fence); human owns the bar (_proposed clause/is_critical/cut scores until confirmed); copyright wall (clause references, not licensed standard text). Blocked on Open Decision 1 — the approved corpus (contents, coverage of the 14 courses, curator). When built, the agent + the AI course tutor are registered in the agent roster + capabilities guide.

8. API surface (/api/v1/lms/*, plus /admin/users/* grants)

Group Routes (method) Capability
Catalog GET/POST /courses, GET /courses/{id}, POST /courses/{id}/modules, POST /modules/{id}/lessons, POST /courses/{id}/status (draft/retired only — publish gated) view / CEO
Delivery POST /courses/{id}/enroll, GET /enrollments, …/lessons/{lid}/complete, …/complete view (own)
Written GET/POST /courses/{id}/quizzes, POST /quizzes/{id}/questions, GET /quizzes/{id}, …/quizzes/{qid}/attempt view / CEO
Certs GET/POST /curricula, GET/POST /certifications, …/{id}/eligibility, …/{id}/issue, GET /credentials[/{id}], …/revoke, POST /credentials/external (lms_extcred), /admin/credentials, /admin/credentials/expire-sweep view / CEO / attestor
Practical GET/POST /rubrics, …/criteria, POST /practicals[/{id}/results|/submit], GET /practicals[/assessing|/{id}] authoring CEO; conduct lms_assess
Competency registry GET/POST /competencies, GET/POST /competencies/{id} (update), …/ratify, …/prereqs, POST /criteria|questions/{id}/competency view / CEO
Matrix/recert GET/PUT/DELETE /competency-matrix…, GET /readiness/{id}?role=, POST /people/{id}/assign-required, GET/POST /standard-revisions, GET /admin/expiring view / self / CEO
Gate PUT /courses/{id}/approval-track, POST /courses/{id}/submit-review, POST /courses/{id}/review, POST /courses/{id}/release, GET /reviews/pending, GET /courses/{id}/approval-log author / track cap / lms_release / view
Records GET /people/{id}/competency-record, GET /admin/records/{id}/history, GET /admin/integrity self / CEO
Grants GET/POST/DELETE /admin/users/{id}/grants CEO

9. Security & threat model

Key properties: no silent publish (API publish path is the gate; direct status → 409); competence ≠ authority (trade approval credential-bound A4; CEO release scoped to "authorized for use" not technical correctness A5; overrides explicit + logged A6); tamper-evidence (all evidence + approval rows chain-anchored; verify_chain() catches direct-DB edits; WORM; retention floor); least privilege (reviewer caps are revocable per-user grants; auditor read-only ahead of CEO override; agent draft-only); one-owner audit line (each competency → one course + clause, schema-enforced).

Attack / failure Result Adequate?
Edit a completion/score/credential via API No such endpoint; completion is server-recomputed ✅
Privileged code edits an lms_* evidence row App paths WORM-guarded; any change breaks the anchor → verify_chain() detects; app-path changes attributed in lms_record_audit ✅ detect + attribute
Delete an attempt/credential/practical/approval row Chain breaks → detected ✅
Backdate/forge a credential issued_at/valid_until set server-side; anchored ✅
Claim practical competence without doing it Requires assessor-bound, attested, locked, anchored rubric record ✅
One-click publish bypassing review Status endpoint 409s on gate states; only the release chain publishes ✅
Trade content approved by a non-credentialed reviewer lms_approve_trade is a per-user grant; A4 blocks approval unless the reviewer's own credential is active ✅
CEO silently publishes rejected content Requires an override action + justification, logged with attribution (A6) ✅
A grant escalates an auditor / overrides a deny Grant check runs after auditor-deny + CEO override; can't subvert them ✅

10. Open items / roadmap

  • P4b authoring agent (blocked on the corpus, §7) and the AI course tutor (P4d).
  • Service-level publish enforcement (defense-in-depth beyond the API boundary).
  • E1 scheduler wiring of expire_due_credentials / expiring_credentials.
  • P3 SCORM/xAPI import (content_type accepts them as placeholders; no runtime yet).
  • P5 onboarding back-fill (feed onboarding_training from completion — closes OQ-12).
  • Content fill of the 14 course shells (gated on the corpus); media hosting + public credential verification.

11. Changelog

  • v3.0 (2026-06-15) — added R-XC-01 (external credentials), R-COMP-01 (competency registry + one-owner invariant + assessment linkage), R-AA-01 P4a (three-stage gate, per-user capability-grants overlay, enforcement). Schema now 22 lms_* tables + capability_grants. Curriculum decisions: OSHA-30 for supervisory roles; tiered blueprint courses (C3 + C16/C17); C13 federal as a third competence domain. PR trail: backend ttis-ai #171–#188, frontend TTI-S_Dashboard #127–#135.
  • v2.0 (2026-06-14) — Q1–Q6 as-built (practical assessment, WORM/retention/audit, competency-record export, prerequisite DAG, competency matrix, recert). PR trail: ttis-ai #161–#170, dash #116–#126.
  • v1.0 — pre-build gap analysis.
